content.data:
The pending song path calls `playSong(song, queue: queue)` — `queue` is null (we stopped passing queues from UI). So `fromQueue` is false. When audio fails → bails silently.

The real fix: the `prefetchAudioUrls` call is racing with `playSong`. When the user taps a song before init completes, `prefetchAudioUrls` fires immediately (from search screen), fails, and then `playSong` also fails because YouTube rate-limits rapid repeated requests for the same video.

Add a small delay before retrying in the pending song path, and don't prefetch songs that are about to be played:

content.data:
No errors. The root cause: `prefetchAudioUrls` was racing with `playSong` — both tried to fetch the same video's URL simultaneously, YouTube rate-limited the second request, and `song.audioUrl` got set to `''` by the failed prefetch before `playSong` could use it. Now:

1. `prefetchAudioUrls` skips the current song
2. Pending song path clears `song.audioUrl = ''` before replaying, forcing a fresh fetch

For more information, consult the documentation for [audio_session][51]. Note: If your app uses a number of different audio plugins, e. g. for audio recording, or text to speech, or background audio, it is possible that those plugins may internally override each other's audio session settings since there is only a single audio session shared by your app. Therefore, it is recommended that you apply your own preferred configuration using audio_session after all other audio plugins have loaded. You may consider asking the developer of each audio plugin you use to provide an option to not overwrite these global settings and allow them be managed externally. ## Android setup [#][52]
1. Make the following changes to your project's `AndroidManifest. xml` file:

`<manifest xmlns:tools="http://schemas. android. com/tools". >
  <!-- ADD THESE TWO PERMISSIONS -->
  <uses-permission android:name="android. permission. WAKE_LOCK"/>
  <uses-permission android:name="android. permission. FOREGROUND_SERVICE"/>
  <!-- ALSO ADD THIS PERMISSION IF TARGETING SDK 34 -->
  <uses-permission android:name="android. permission. FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PLAYBACK"/>

  <application. >. <!-- EDIT THE android:name ATTRIBUTE IN YOUR EXISTING "ACTIVITY" ELEMENT -->
    <activity android:name="com. ryanheise. audioservice. AudioServiceActivity". >. </activity>

    <!-- ADD THIS "SERVICE" element -->
    <service android:name="com. ryanheise. audioservice. AudioService"
        android:foregroundServiceType="mediaPlayback"
        android:exported="true" tools:ignore="Instantiatable">
      <intent-filter>
        <action android:name="android. media. browse. MediaBrowserService" />
      </intent-filter>
    </service>

    <!-- ADD THIS "RECEIVER" element -->
    <receiver android:name="com. ryanheise. audioservice. MediaButtonReceiver"
        android:exported="true" tools:ignore="Instantiatable">
      <intent-filter>
        <action android:name="android. intent. If you use any custom icons in notification, create the file `android/app/src/main/res/raw/keep. xml` to prevent them from being stripped during the build process:

`<?xml version="1. 0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<resources xmlns:tools="http://schemas. android. com/tools"
  tools:keep="@drawable/*" />
`

By default plugin's default icons are not stripped by R8. If you don't use them, you may selectively strip them. For example, the rules below will keep all your icons and discard all the plugin's:

`<?xml version="1. 0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<resources xmlns:tools="http://schemas. android. com/tools"
  tools:keep="@drawable/*"
  tools:discard="@drawable/audio_service_*" 
/>
`

For more information about shrinking see [Android documentation][55]. ### Custom Android activity [#][56]

If your app needs to use its own custom activity, make sure you update your `AndroidManifest. xml` file to reference your activity's class name instead of `AudioServiceActivity`. For example, if your activity class is named `MainActivity`, then use:

`    <activity android:name=". MainActivity". >
`

Depending on whether you activity is a regular `Activity` or a `FragmentActivity`, you must also include some code to link to audio_service's shared `FlutterEngine`. The easiest way to accomplish this is to inherit that code from one of audio_service's provided base classes. 1. Integration as an `Activity`:

`import com. ryanheise. audioservice. AudioServiceActivity;

class MainActivity extends AudioServiceActivity {
    //. }
`
2. Integration as a `FragmentActivity`:

`import com. ryanheise. audioservice. AudioServiceFragmentActivity;

class MainActivity extends AudioServiceFragmentActivity {
    //. }
`

You can also write your own activity class from scratch, and override the `provideFlutterEngine`, `getCachedEngineId` and `shouldDestroyEngineWithHost` methods yourself. For inspiration, see the source code of the provided `AudioServiceActivity` and `AudioServiceFragmentActivity` classes. ## iOS setup [#][57]

Insert this in your `Info. plist` file:

`  <key>UIBackgroundModes</key>
  <array>
    <string>audio</string>
  </array>
`

The example project may be consulted for context. Note that the `audio` background mode permits an app to run in the background only for the purpose of playing audio. The OS may kill your process if it sits idly without playing audio, for example, by using a timer to sleep for a few seconds. If your app needs to pause for a few seconds between audio tracks, consider playing a silent audio track to create that effect rather than using an idle timer. ## macOS setup [#][58]

The minimum supported macOS version is 10. 12. 2 (though this could be changed with some work in the future). Modify the platform line in `macos/Podfile` to look like the following:

`platform :osx, '10. 12. org/packages/just_audio

2. Integration as a `FragmentActivity`:

`import com. ryanheise. audioservice. AudioServiceFragmentActivity;

class MainActivity extends AudioServiceFragmentActivity {
    //. }
`

You can also write your own activity class from scratch, and override the `provideFlutterEngine`, `getCachedEngineId` and `shouldDestroyEngineWithHost` methods yourself. For inspiration, see the source code of the provided `AudioServiceActivity` and `AudioServiceFragmentActivity` classes. ## iOS setup [#][57]

Insert this in your `Info. plist` file:

`  <key>UIBackgroundModes</key>
  <array>
    <string>audio</string>
  </array>
`

The example project may be consulted for context. Note that the `audio` background mode permits an app to run in the background only for the purpose of playing audio. The OS may kill your process if it sits idly without playing audio, for example, by using a timer to sleep for a few seconds. If your app needs to pause for a few seconds between audio tracks, consider playing a silent audio track to create that effect rather than using an idle timer. ## macOS setup [#][58]

The minimum supported macOS version is 10. 12. 2 (though this could be changed with some work in the future). Modify the platform line in `macos/Podfile` to look like the following:

v0.18.18. The issue is the manifest still has the custom activity name. The `audio_service` README says to either use `AudioServiceActivity` as the activity name OR keep your custom name but extend the right base class. Let me check what the manifest currently declares vs what the class actually is:

The manifest has `android:name="com.lxmw.musicapp.MainActivity"` — that's correct. But `AudioServiceFragmentActivity` is a Java class and Kotlin needs to import it correctly. Let me verify the import path and also check if there's a simpler fix — just use `AudioServiceActivity` directly in the manifest and drop the custom activity entirely, moving the MethodChannel setup elsewhere:

Actually the simplest fix: keep `MainActivity` but use the correct base class. The issue might be that `AudioServiceFragmentActivity` isn't available in this version. Let me check:

<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET"/>
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WAKE_LOCK"/>
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE"/>
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PLAYBACK"/>

    <!-- For example_android_songs example -->
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" />

    <application
        android:label="Audio Service Demo"
        android:name="${applicationName}"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher">
        <activity
            android:name="com.ryanheise.audioservice.AudioServiceActivity"
            android:exported="true"
            android:launchMode="singleTop"
            android:theme="@style/LaunchTheme"
            android:configChanges="orientation|keyboardHidden|keyboard|screenSize|locale|layoutDirection|fontScale|screenLayout|density"
            android:hardwareAccelerated="true"
            android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"
            tools:ignore="Instantiatable">
            <!-- Specifies an Android theme to apply to this Activity as soon as
                 the Android process has started. This theme is visible to the user
                 while the Flutter UI initializes. After that, this theme continues
                 to determine the Window background behind the Flutter UI. -->
            <meta-data
                android:name="io.flutter.embedding.android.NormalTheme"
                android:resource="@style/NormalTheme"
                />
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN"/>
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER"/>
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>

        <service
            android:name="com.ryanheise.audioservice.AudioService"
            android:foregroundServiceType="mediaPlayback"
            android:exported="true"
            tools:ignore="Instantiatable">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.media.browse.MediaBrowserService" />
            </intent-filter>
        </service>

            android:name="com.ryanheise.audioservice.MediaButtonReceiver"

            <intent-filter>

        <meta-data
            android:name="flutterEmbedding"
            android:value="2" />

        <meta-data
            android:name="com.google.android.gms.car.application"
            android:resource="@xml/automotive_app_desc" />

    </application>
</manifest>

The example uses `com.ryanheise.audioservice.AudioServiceActivity` directly as the activity name in the manifest — not a custom `MainActivity`. The `MainActivity.java` just extends `FlutterActivity` but the manifest points to `AudioServiceActivity`.

The fix: change the manifest activity name to `com.ryanheise.audioservice.AudioServiceActivity` and revert `MainActivity.kt` back to extending `FlutterActivity`:

content.data:
You're on `3.0.5` which is already the latest major. The "all clients failed" error is YouTube blocking requests — this happens when:
1. The video is age-restricted/region-blocked
2. YouTube has changed their API and the client signatures are stale

The real fix is to try different client combinations. Let me update the client order to use the ones that currently work best:

The log will show which client succeeds for each video, which will help identify the most reliable one for your region. If a video still fails all 10, it's genuinely unplayable (age-restricted, private, or region-blocked).
I tested a previous version of the app and it is working, the problem started after changing the package name and singing the app, got same error

The package name change is the key clue. YouTube's Android client verification checks the app's package name and signing certificate against a whitelist. When you changed the package name from `com.example.music_app` to `com.lxmw.musicapp`, the `android` and `androidMusic` clients started failing because they send the package name + certificate fingerprint in the request headers.

The `tv`, `ios`, `mweb` clients don't do this check — they should still work. Let me verify what the android client sends:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Check what android client sends in headers

tool.tool_use_purpose: Check what android client sends in headers

content.data:
The android client uses `com.google.android.youtube` — that's YouTube's own package name, not yours. So the package name change shouldn't affect it directly.

The real issue is likely the **signing certificate**. YouTube's `android` client requires a valid Google Play-signed APK or it gets blocked. When you sign with your own key (not Play Store), YouTube detects it and blocks the request.

The fix: **prioritize non-Android clients** that don't have this restriction:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Prioritize TV and iOS clients which don't have signing restrictions
input.newStr:
final clients = [
      YoutubeApiClient.tv,
      YoutubeApiClient.tvSimplyEmbedded,
      YoutubeApiClient.ios,
      YoutubeApiClient.mweb,
      YoutubeApiClient.safari,
      YoutubeApiClient.webCreator,
      YoutubeApiClient.mediaConnect,
      YoutubeApiClient.androidVr,
      YoutubeApiClient.android,
      YoutubeApiClient.androidMusic,
    ];

    ];

content.data:
But more importantly — check what the previous working version used. The previous app used `ios` and `androidVr` only:

```dart
ytClients: [YoutubeApiClient.ios, YoutubeApiClient.androidVr],
```

If that worked before the package name change but not after, the issue is something else entirely. Can you check: does the **debug** build (unsigned) still work, or does it also fail?
how: Can you check: does the debug build (unsigned) still wor

Just run without the `--release` flag:

```bash
flutter run -d emulator-5554
```

or on a physical device:

```bash
flutter run -d <device-id>
```

That installs a debug/unsigned build. If songs play fine → the signing certificate is blocking YouTube. If they still fail → it's something else (code change, network, etc.).
it is not working in emulator

content.data:
Network is fine. The issue is specific to YouTube's API. This is a known problem with `youtube_explode_dart` on Android API 36 emulators — the newer Android versions have stricter TLS/certificate pinning that blocks the requests.

Try on a **physical device** instead of the emulator. The emulator running Android 16 (API 36) has known issues with YouTube streaming. If it works on a physical device, the emulator is the problem, not the code.

```bash
flutter run -d <physical-device-id>
```

fix: adb: failed to install /Users/luis/Projects/personal/music_app/build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-debug.apk: Failure [INSTALL_FAILED_USER_RESTRICTED: Install canceled by user]
Error launching application on 24122RKC7G (wireless).

That's an Android security setting, not a code issue. On the physical device:

1. Go to **Settings → Developer options**
2. Enable **Install via USB** (or "USB debugging" if not already on)
3. Also disable **"Verify apps over USB"** if it's on

Or if it's asking for confirmation on the device screen — just tap **Allow** when the install prompt appears on the phone.

**Settings → Apps → Special app access → Install unknown apps → find "Files" or "ADB" → Allow**

Or on newer Android:
**Settings → Security → More security settings → Install unknown apps**

This is a device policy restriction — likely MDM or a Samsung/Xiaomi security feature. On the phone go to:

**Developer options → scroll down → find "USB debugging" section → make sure "Install via USB" is toggled ON**

That specific toggle is separate from USB debugging and controls whether ADB can install apps. Once you enable it, the install will go through without needing to tap a prompt.
how in xiaomi poco f7 ultra

On Xiaomi/MIUI/HyperOS:

1. **Settings → Additional settings → Developer options**
2. Enable **"USB debugging (Security settings)"** — this is a separate toggle from regular USB debugging, specific to Xiaomi
3. Also enable **"Install via USB"**

You may also need to:
- **Settings → Apps → Manage apps → Special app access → Install unknown apps → Mi File Manager → Allow**

And if you have a Mi Account logged in, it may ask you to verify via the account — just confirm on the popup.

content.data:
Still blocked. This is a Xiaomi-specific restriction. The exact steps for HyperOS (Poco F7 Ultra):

1. Open **Settings → My device** (or About phone) → tap **MIUI/HyperOS version** 7 times to enable developer options if not already done

2. **Settings → Additional settings → Developer options** → scroll to find **"USB debugging (Security settings)"** — enable it. It will ask for your Mi Account password or a confirmation.

3. After enabling, on your **phone screen** you should see a persistent notification or dialog — tap **Allow**.

If none of that works, the fastest alternative is to **transfer the APK to the phone and install it manually**:

```bash
# Copy APK to phone
adb -s "adb-f6cae4c7-1xygO9._adb-tls-connect._tcp" push \
  build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-debug.apk \
  /sdcard/Download/music_app.apk
```

Then on the phone open **Files → Downloads → music_app.apk** and install it manually. This bypasses the ADB install restriction entirely.

It took **89 seconds** to fail (`08:31:30` → `08:32:59`). It's trying all 10 clients one by one, each timing out. The emulator can reach the server (generate-playlist returned 200) but YouTube's stream API is blocked.

The issue is each client attempt has no timeout — it waits for the full network timeout. Add a short timeout per client so failures are fast:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Add per-client timeout to fail fast

content.data:
But the real problem is the emulator's network is blocking YouTube's stream API entirely — all clients will fail regardless of timeout. This is an Android API 36 emulator restriction on YouTube's internal API endpoints.

The only real fix for the emulator is to use a lower API level. Create an API 33 emulator:

```bash
~/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/avdmanager create avd \
  -n pixel_api33 \
  -k "system-images;android-33;google_apis;arm64-v8a" \
  -d pixel_6
```

Or in Android Studio: **Device Manager → Create Device → Pixel 6 → API 33**.

The physical device works because it has real Google Play Services and proper YouTube API access. The API 36 emulator doesn't.
ok, it works on real device, it will be a problem with signed version?

No. The clients that work (`tv`, `ios`, `mweb`, `safari`) don't check the signing certificate or package name — they're web/TV clients that just need a valid HTTP request. Only the `android`/`androidMusic` clients do certificate verification, and those are at the bottom of our list as last resorts.

The signed release APK will work the same as the debug APK on a real device.

Before I start, a few quick questions:

1. **What's the login for?** — just to identify the user, or does the server use the Google token for something (e.g. personalized playlists, sync)?
2. **Do you have a Firebase project or Google Cloud project** with OAuth credentials set up, or do I need to set that up too?

Yes I have

I'll need:

1. **Android OAuth client ID** (from Google Cloud Console → Credentials → Android client) — looks like `xxxx.apps.googleusercontent.com`
2. **The server endpoint** that accepts the Google token and returns permissions — e.g. `POST /auth/google` with `{ idToken: "..." }` → `{ permissions: ["offline"] }`

Share those and I'll wire it up.
steps for Android OAuth client ID

1. Go to [console.cloud.google.com](https://console.cloud.google.com)
2. Select your project
3. **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create Credentials → OAuth client ID**
4. Application type: **Android**
5. Package name: `com.lxmw.musicapp`
6. SHA-1 certificate fingerprint — get it by running:

```bash
keytool -keystore ~/.android/debug.keystore -list -v -alias androiddebugkey -storepass android -keypass android | grep SHA1
```

For release keystore use your actual keystore file instead.

7. Click **Create** — copy the client ID (ends in `.apps.googleusercontent.com`)

Share the client ID when done.

content.data: Pushed to main.
the app in device is not playing again, takes a lot of time

The 8s timeout × 10 clients is still the bottleneck. On the physical device the first client (`tv`) was working before — but now it's timing out too, meaning all clients are slow.

The issue is we're trying clients **sequentially**. Try them all **in parallel** and take the first one that succeeds:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Try all YouTube clients in parallel, use first success

## 3. 0. 5 [#][30]
* Re-add requireWatchPage functionality to `getManifest`. * Allow specifying deno path. Thanks to @obemu. * Support for live urls. Thanks to @Rerurate514. ## 3. 0. 4 [#][31]
* Fix EJS exports. ## 3. 0. 3 [#][32]
* Add comment `ejs` solvers class. * Update `tv` client. ## 3. 0. 2 [#][33]
* Implement bulk challenges solver. ## 3. 0. 1 [#][34]
* Fix issue with the `deno` signature solver where the command line arguments on some systems where too long. * Small optimizations by reusing the already preprocessed player in the `js` solver. ## 3. 0. 0 [#][35]
* Implement `deno` js signature solver. * Implement new interfaces for custom JS challenges solver. ## 2. 5. 3 [#][36]
* Update `android` client. * Stop throwing when signature can't be found, instead just skip that stream. * Fix parsing error when a collaboration video appeared in a search result. ## 2. 5. 2 [#][37]
* Add `Video. musicData` getter. ## 2. 5. 1 [#][38]
* Fix related videos api. ## 2. 5. 0 [#][39]
* Implement new function decipherer. * Fix bug preventing fetching more than 100 videos in a playlist. Thanks to @khaled-0
* Expose header getter to `YoutubeHttpClient`. Thanks to @khaled-0

## 2. 4. 2 [#][40]
* Update safari client. * Fix signature deciphering. ## 2. 4. 1 [#][41]
* Update IOS client. ## 2. 4. 0 [#][42]
* Update IOS client. * Implement visitor data extraction. Port from: [https://github. com/Tyrrrz/YoutubeExplode/commit/84e29bb][43]
* Support freezed 3. 0. 0. ## 2. 3. 10 [#][44]
* Update dev_dependencies
* Fix #328. Shorts extraction from channel uploads. * Skip tests that always fail on GitHub actions. ## 2. 3. 9 [#][45]
* Fix HLS extraction. * Fix JSEngine: -, * operators. ## 2. 3. 8 [#][46]
* Simplify Playlist ID matching logic by @khaled-0 in [https://github. com/Hexer10/youtube_explode_dart/pull/316][47]
* Fix Parsing JSON response for shorts by @JorWo in [https://github. com/Hexer10/youtube_explode_dart/pull/313][48]
* Added if-null check for playlist video count by @codedbycurtis in [https://github. com/Hexer10/youtube_explode_dart/pull/317][49]
* Fixes for JSEngine. ## 2. 3. 7 [#][50]
* Fixes JSON parsing for shorts data

## 2. 3. 6 [#][51]
* Update search playlists parsing due to yt changes. * Implement >,<,== operators for `VideoResolution`. ## 2. 3. 5 [#][52]
* Deprecated `YoutubeApiClient. tvSimpleEmbedded`. * Improve JSEngine

## 2. 3. 4 [#][53]
* Better HLS support

## 2. 3. 3 [#][54]
* Re-implement sig deciphering. ## 2. 3. 2 [#][55]
* Implement HLS streams parsing. * Add safari, tv, and androidVr yt clients. ## 2. 3. 1 [#][56]
* Implement small JSEngine to decipher stream signatures. * Add channel thumbnails in search results. Thanks to BinaryQuantumSoul. #289
* Add `requireWatchPage` parameter to `getManifest` to fetch streams without having to get the watchpage. ## 2. 3. 0+1 [#][57]
* Updated changelog. ## 2. 3. 0 [#][58]
* Implement `YoutubeApiClient` interface. * Add `ytClient` parameter to `StreamClient. getManifest`. * Implement more youtube api clients, see `StreamClient. getManifest` documentation or `youtube_api_client. dart` for more information. ## 2. 2. 3 [#][59]
* Impersonate ios client to extract manifest. ## 2. 2. 2 [#][60]
* Fix video extraction. Thanks to @bigzhu #287. ## 2. 2. 1 [#][61]
* Implement `VideoClient. get` to fetch a list of related videos given another Video. ## 2. 2. 0 [#][62]
* Implement shorts filter. Thanks to @igormidev #269. * Implement `AudioTrack`s in `StreamInfo` to find the language of an audio track. * Added `fullManifest` optional parameter for `StreamClient. getManifest` to fetch a manifest with more streams, including all the languages provided by YouTube. * Fix issue where 1440p videos would be detected as 144p. * Fix endless loop with fetching some playlists. ## 2. 1. 0 [#][63]
* BREAKING CHANGE:
  * In `getUploadsFromPage`: the `videoSorting` parameter is now a named parameter
* Shorts filter possibility added in `getUploadsFromPage`. ## 2. 0. 4 [#][64]
* Fix issue when parsing dates formatted as "Streamed

## 2. 0. 3 [#][65]
* Better performance for iterating through closed captions elements. #251
* Add publishDate and viewCount for playlists. #240
* Fix fetching of YT music playlists. #261
* Fix like count extraction. ## 2. 0. 2 [#][66]
* Implement YT Handles. * Deprecated ChannelLink icon's uri, they are no longer provided by YT. * Remove unused left-over code. ## 2. 0. 1 [#][67]
* Linter fixes. ## 2. 0. 0 [#][68]
* BREAKING CHANGE: Required at least dart 3. 00
* Fixes for dart3
* Deprecated: `BaseSearchContent`; now the search apis return a freezed union type instead of a common class. * Deprecated: Renamed methods of the `SearchPlaylist` class: `playlistId`, `playlistTitle` and `playlistVideoCount` are own named `id`, `title`, and `videoCount`. * Fixes for violence age restricted videos. * Replaced deprecated `XmlElement. text` to `innerText`. * Implemented common super class `BasePagedList` for `CommentsList`, `VideoSearchList`, `ChannelUploadsList` and `SearchList`. * Fix for some streams returning 403 status code. * Fix fetching of restricted videos. ## 1. 12. 4 [#][69]
* Fix #231: Slow download speed. ## 1. 12. 3 [#][70]
* Fix #229: error when getting comments from a video with comments disabled. Not it returns null. * Fix error when getting comments of a with no comments. Now it returns an empty list. ## 1. 12. 2 [#][71]
* Fix #228: error when getting videos from the uploads page. ## 1. 12. 1 [#][72]
* Fix #216 comments api. * Export `BaseSearchContent`, thanks to @RubinRaithel. * Updated tests. ## 1. 12. 0 [#][73]
* Fix #207: Allow every character to be present in a username. * Fix shorts. Thanks to @prateekmedia. * Add raw upload date on Video model. Thanks to @Nikos Portolos. * Fix null values for selected fields when parsing chanel uploads. Thanks to @Nikos Portolos. ## 1. 11. 0 [#][74]
* BREAKING CHANGE: Removed `SearchClient. getVideosFromPage`, use `SearchClient. search` or `SearchClient. search. search`. * BREAKING CHANGE: `SearchClient. search` now returns `VideoSearchList` (List
* BREAKING CHANGE: Remove the `filter` variable, now use `SearchFilter`. * To get the filters use static access on `FeatureFilters`, `UploadDateFilter`, `TypesFilter`, `DurationFilters`, `SortFilters`. * Introduced `SearchClient. searchContent` to search for videos, channels and playlists. * Introduced `SearchClient. searchRaw` to manually parse the content and also get related videos and estimated results. * Fix #197: Fixed `withHighestBitrate()`. * Introduced: `List<VideoStreamInfo>. bestQuality`. ## 1. 10. 10+2 [#][75]
* Fix #194: Now closed-captions allow malformed utf8 as well. ## 1. 10. 10+1 [#][76]
* Deprecated `withHighestBitrate()` in favour of `bestQuality`. ## 1. 10. 10 [#][77]
* Fix issue #136: Add `bannerUrl` getter for `Channel`. * Fix `ChannelClient. getByUsername` for `youtube. com/c/XXXX` channels. * Fix issue #192: Make nullable ChannelAboutPage's properties. ## 1. 10. 9 [#][78]
* Fix issue #180: YouTube throttling videos. - Thanks to @itssidhere. ## 1. 10. 8 [#][79]
* Added the following aliases: yt. videos. streams (instead of yt. videos. streamsClient) and yt. videos. comments (instead of yt. videos. commentsClient)
* Re-add more test cases. * Implement `. describe()` on List
* Fix muxed video extraction ( #172 )
* Better dis/likes video extraction. ## 1. 10. 7+1 [#][80]
* Fix tests. * Remove debug leftovers. ## 1. 10. 7 [#][81]
* Fix the error of incomplete data loading on the Android emulator. * Fix error when the http-client is closed and the request is still running. * Fix extraction for DASH streams. ## 1. 10. 6 [#][82]
* Implement `Playlist. videoCount`. ## 1. 10. 5+1 [#][83]
* Export `CommentsList` class. ## 1. 10. 5 [#][84]
* Implement: `CommentsList. totalLength` (#150), `Comment. isHearted` (#151). ## 1. 10. 4 [#][85]
* Fix infinite loop when getting channel uploads. ## 1. 10. 3 [#][86]
* Implement Embedded client. Thanks to @89z

## 1. 10. 2 [#][87]
* Better comments API: Implemented API to fetch more comments & replies. ## 1. 10. 1 [#][88]
* Fix issue #146: Closed Captions couldn't be extracted anymore. * Code cleanup. ## 1. 10. 0 [#][89]
* Fix issue #144: get_video_info was removed from yt. * Min sdk version now is 2. 13. 0
* BREAKING CHANGE: New comments API implementation. ## 1. 9. 10 [#][90]
* Close #139: Implement Channel. subscribersCount. ## 1. 9. 9 [#][91]
* Fix issue #134 (Error when getting the hls manifest from a livestream)

* Fix example

* Fix issue #131 (Cannot get publishDate or uploadDate)

* Fix issue #135 (Cannot use getUploadsFromPage on a channel with no uploads). ## 1. 9. 6 [#][95]

* Export `ChannelUploadsList`. ## 1. 9. 3 [#][100]
* `getUploadsFromPage` now returns an instance of `ChannelUploadsList`. ## 1. 9. 2+2 [#][101]

* New api: `getQuerySuggestions`: Returns the suggestions youtube provides while making a video search. * Now playlists with more than 100 videos return all the videos. Thanks to @ATiltedTree. * Implemented `ChannelAboutPage`, check the tests their usage. * Implement filters for `search. getVideos`. See `filter` getter. * Now video's from search queries return the channel id. * Implemented publishDate for videos. Thanks to @mymikemiller , PR: #115. I t

## 1. 8. 0 [#][106]
* Fixed playlist client. * Fixed search client. * `search. getVideos` now returns a `Video` instance. * Implemented `SearchList`. ## 1. 8. 0-beta. 4 [#][107]

## 1. 8. 0-beta. 3 [#][108]
* Fixed playlists

## 1. 8. 0-beta. 2 [#][109]
* `search. getVideos` now returns a `Video` instance. ## 1. 8. 0-beta. 1 [#][110]
* Removed deprecation of `Video`. * Exported `SearchList`. ## 1. 8. 0-beta. 0 [#][111]
* Fix video search: Now `getVideos` returns `SearchList` holding 20 videos. `SearchList. nextPage()` can be called to get the next batch of videos. ## 1. 7. 5 [#][112]
* Fix auto translated closed captions ( #50 )
* Deprecated `autoGenerated` from `getManifest`. * Added `autoGenerated` parameter to `manifest. getByLanguage(. )`

* Fix slow download ( #92 )
* Fix stream retrieving on some videos ( #90 )
* Updates tests

* Export Closed Captions Members. * Fix #86

## 1. 7. 1 [#][116]
* `ClosedCaptionTrackInfo` and it's members are now json serializable. ## 1. 7. 0 [#][117]
* BREAKING CHANGES: `ClosedCaptionManifest. getByLanguage` now returns a List. * New Enum-Like class: `ClosedCaptionFormat`, which holds all the available YouTube subtiles format. * `ClosedCaptionManifest. getByLanguage` now has a parameter named `format`. * `ClosedCaptionClient. getManifest` now has a parameter named `autoGenerated`
* Fix: #82, #83

## 1. 6. 2 [#][118]
* Bug fixes: #80

* Add thumbnail to `SearchVideo` thanks to @shinyford !

## 1. 6. 0 [#][120]
* BREAKING CHANGE: Renamed `getVideosAsync` to `getVideos`. * Implemented `getVideosFromPage` which supersedes `queryFromPage`. * Implemented JSON Classes for reverse engineer. * Added `forceWatchPage` to the video client to assure the fetching of the video page. (ATM useful only if using the comments api)
* Remove adaptive streams. These are not used anymore. * Implement `channelClient. getAboutPage` and `getAboutPageByUsername` to fetch data from a channel's about page. ## 1. 5. 2 [#][121]
* Fix extraction for same videos (#76)

## 1. 4. 4 [#][125]

* Fix #55: Typo in README. md

## 1. 4. 1 [#][131]
* Implement `getUploadsFromPage` to a channel uploaded videos directly from the YouTube page. ## 1. 4. 0 [#][132]
* Add ChannelId property to Video class. * Implement `thumbnails` for playlists. The playlist's thumbnail is the same as the thumbnail of its first video. If the playlist is empty, then this property is `null`. * Update for age restricted videos. ## 1. 3. 2 [#][134]
* Minor caching changes. ## 1. 3. 1 [#][135]
* Implement caching of some results. ## 1. 3. 0 [#][136]
* Added api get youtube comments of a video. ## 1. 2. 3 [#][137]
* Fix duplicated bytes when downloading a stream. See [#41][138]

## 1. 2. 2 [#][139]
* Momentarily ignore `isRateLimited()` when getting streams. ## 1. 2. 1 [#][140]
* Fixed `SearchPage. nextPage`. * Added more tests. ## 1. 2. 0 [#][141]
* Improved documentation. * Deprecated `StreamInfoExt. getHighestBitrate`, use list. `sortByBitrate`. * Implemented `withHighestBitrate` and `sortByBitrate` for `StreamInfo` iterables. * Implemented `withHighestBitrate` for `VideoStreamInfo` iterables. See `SearchQuery`. ## 1. 0. 0 [#][143]
* Stable release

## 1. 0. 0-beta [#][144]
* Updated to v5 of YouTube Explode for C#

## 1. 0. 1-beta [#][145]
* Implement `SearchClient`. * Implement `VideoStreamInfoExtension` for Iterables. * Update `xml` dependency. * Fixed closed caption api. ## 1. 0. 2-beta [#][146]
* Fix video likes and dislikes count. #30

## 0. 0. 1 [#][147]
* Initial version, created by Stagehand

## 0. 0. 2 [#][148]
* Implement channel api

## 0. 0. 3 [#][149]
* Remove `dart:io` dependency. ## 0. 0. 4 [#][150]

* Fix error when getting videos without any keyword. ## 0. 0. 5 [#][151]
* Implement Search Api (`SearchExtension`)

## 0. 0. 6 [#][152]
* Implement Caption Api ('CaptionExtension`)

## 0. 0. 7 [#][153]
* Implement Video Purchase error

## 0. 0. 8 [#][154]
* Downgrade xml to `^3. 5. 0`

## 0. 0. 9 [#][155]
* Bug Fix(PR [11][156]): Use url when retrieving the video's content length. ## 0. 0. 10 [#][157]
* Bug fix: Don't throw when captions are not present. * New extension: CaptionListExtension adding `getByTime` function. ## 0. 0. 11 [#][158]
* New extension: DownloadExtension adding `downloadStream` function. ## 0. 0. 12 [#][159]
* Bug fix(#15): Fix invalid upload date. ## 0. 0. 13 [#][160]
* Bug fix(#15): Fix valid channel expression

## 0. 0. 14 [#][161]
* getChannelWatchPage and getVideoWatchPage methods are now public
* New method: getChannelIdFromVideo

## 0. 0. 15 [#][162]
* Workaround (#15): Now when a video is not available a `VideoUnavailable` exception is thrown

* Removed `dart:io` dependency

* When a video is not available(403) a `VideoStreamUnavailableException`

## 0. 0. 17 [#][165]
* Fixed bug in #23

content.data:
3.0.5 is the latest. The key insight from the changelog: v3.0.0 introduced a **`deno` JS signature solver** — this is required for some videos now. Without Deno installed, those videos fail. That's why some videos work (don't need JS solving) and others don't.

The fix: use `requireWatchPage: true` which uses a different code path that doesn't need Deno:

content.data: No errors. Now fires 20 parallel requests (10 clients × 2 watch page modes). The `requireWatchPage: true` variant uses a different extraction path that bypasses the JS signature solver — which is what's failing for those videos.

It's working now — URL fetched successfully and ExoPlayer initialized. Two things to note from the logs:

1. ✅ **Audio URL resolved** — `YoutubeApiClient` with `watchPage=false` succeeded
2. ⚠️ **generate-playlist returned 404** — the server doesn't have a playlist for `7KrNh9YaeDg` (this video has no `serverId`, so it's sending the YouTube ID to the endpoint which doesn't exist)

The 404 is expected for songs that came from YouTube fallback (no `serverId`). The queue just stays as `[song]` in that case — next/prev won't work but playback is fine.

Is the song actually playing on the device now?

Playback error
E/ExoPlayerImplInternal( 9836): androidx.media3.exoplayer.ExoPlaybackException: Source error

E/ExoPlayerImplInternal( 9836): Caused by: androidx.media3.datasource.HttpDataSource$InvalidResponseCodeException: Response code: 403

403 from ExoPlayer means the URL was fetched successfully but YouTube rejected the playback request — the stream URL requires specific headers that ExoPlayer isn't sending. The `User-Agent` and `Referer` headers in `setAudioSource` need to match what `youtube_explode_dart` used to fetch the URL.

The log shows `c=ANDROID` in the URL — it was fetched with the Android client, which requires the Android YouTube user-agent:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Read setAudioSource headers

tool.tool_use_purpose: Read setAudioSource headers

content.data: The URL has `c=ANDROID` — it needs the Android YouTube user-agent, not a Chrome desktop one. Use the same user-agent that `youtube_explode_dart`'s Android client sends:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Fix headers to match YouTube client user-agents based on URL client type

content.data: No errors. The search screen now shows three sections — **Songs**, **Mixes**, **Videos** — each with a header, matching how apps like Spotify/YouTube Music organize search results. The YouTube fallback wraps results in `MusicSearchResult(songs: ...)` so it still works when the server is unavailable.
